Topic: List

append - Adds one item to the end of the list.
extend - Adds all items from another iterable to the end of the list.
insert - Inserts an item at a specific index.
remove - Removes the first matching item by value.
pop - Removes and returns an item by index (last item by default).
clear - Removes all items from the list.
index - Returns the index of the first matching item.
count - Returns how many times a value appears in the list.
sort - Sorts the list in place (ascending by default).
reverse - Reverses the order of items in place.
copy - Returns a shallow copy of the list.

Topic: Tuple

tuple (overview) - Ordered, immutable collection that allows duplicate values.
count - Returns how many times a value appears in the tuple.
index - Returns the index of the first matching value.
packing - Combines multiple values into a single tuple.
unpacking - Assigns tuple values to multiple variables.
extended unpacking (*) - Captures multiple middle values into a list.
tuple() - Converts an iterable (like list) into a tuple.

Topic: Set

set (overview) - Unordered collection of unique values. No duplicates allowed.
add - Adds one item to the set.
update - Adds multiple items from an iterable to the set.
remove - Removes an item by value. Raises error if not found.
discard - Removes an item safely. No error if value does not exist.
pop - Removes and returns an arbitrary item (order is unpredictable).
clear - Removes all items from the set.
copy - Returns a shallow copy of the set.
union (|) - Returns all unique items from both sets.
intersection (&) - Returns only items common to both sets.
difference (-) - Returns items in first set but not in second.
symmetric difference (^) - Returns items in either set but not both.
issubset - Checks if all items of one set exist in another.
issuperset - Checks if a set contains all items of another.
isdisjoint - Returns True if two sets share no common items.
set() - Converts an iterable to a set (removes duplicates).

Topic: Frozenset

frozenset (overview) - Immutable, unordered collection of unique values.
frozenset() - Creates an immutable set from an iterable.
in - Fast membership check in frozenset.
| (union) - Returns all unique items from both frozensets.
& (intersection) - Returns common items from both frozensets.
- (difference) - Returns items in first frozenset but not second.
^ (symmetric difference) - Returns items in either frozenset but not both.
issubset - Checks if all values of one frozenset exist in another.
issuperset - Checks if one frozenset contains all values of another.
isdisjoint - Returns True when two collections have no common values.
use case - Can be used as dictionary keys and inside sets because it is hashable.

Topic: Hashable & Unhashable

hashable (overview) - Value with stable hash; can be used in set and as dict key.
unhashable (overview) - Value without stable hash; cannot be used in set or as dict key.
hashable examples - int, str, bool, tuple (with hashable items), frozenset.
unhashable examples - list, dict, set.
key rule - Mutable types are usually unhashable; immutable types are usually hashable.
tuple rule - Tuple is hashable only if all inner elements are hashable.
nested set rule - Use frozenset inside set because regular set is unhashable.
hash() - Built-in function used to check hashability (raises TypeError for unhashable values).

Topic: Strings

string (overview) - Ordered, immutable sequence of characters. UTF-8 encoded.
indexing - Access char by position. s[0] = first, s[-1] = last.
slicing - s[start:stop:step]. s[::-1] reverses string.
len() - Returns total number of characters in string.
concatenation (+) - Joins two strings. Cannot use with int directly.
repetition (*) - Repeats string n times. msg * 3 prints msg 3 times.
input() - Always returns string. Use int(input()) for number input.
int() / str() / float() - Type conversion between string and numbers.
upper() - Converts all characters to uppercase.
lower() - Converts all characters to lowercase.
title() - Capitalizes first letter of every word.
capitalize() - Capitalizes only first letter of string.
swapcase() - Swaps upper to lower and lower to upper.
strip() - Removes leading and trailing whitespace (or given chars).
lstrip() - Removes only leading (left) whitespace or chars.
rstrip() - Removes only trailing (right) whitespace or chars.
replace(old, new) - Replaces all occurrences of old with new.
count(sub) - Counts how many times a substring appears.
find(sub) - Returns index of first match, or -1 if not found (safe).
index(sub) - Returns index of first match, raises ValueError if not found.
startswith(prefix) - Returns True if string starts with given prefix.
endswith(suffix) - Returns True if string ends with given suffix.
split(sep) - Splits string into a list by separator (default: whitespace).
join(iterable) - Joins list items into a string using separator.
splitlines() - Splits multiline string into list of lines.
removeprefix(pre) - Removes prefix if present, else returns string unchanged.
removesuffix(suf) - Removes suffix if present, else returns string unchanged.
isdigit() - Returns True if all characters are digits.
isalpha() - Returns True if all characters are letters.
isalnum() - Returns True if all characters are letters or digits.
isspace() - Returns True if all characters are whitespace.
isupper() / islower() - Checks if string is all upper or lower case.
center(width, char) - Centers string padded with char to given width.
ljust() / rjust() - Left or right justifies string within given width.
zfill(width) - Pads string with zeros on the left to given width.
f-string - f"text {var}" - Inline variable interpolation and formatting.
f-string :.2f - Formats float to 2 decimal places. :.4f for 4 places.
f-string = - f"{var=}" prints variable name and value for debugging.
raw string r"" - Treats backslashes as literal characters.
in operator - Checks if substring exists in string. "a" in "abc" -> True.
escape sequences - \n newline, \t tab, \\ backslash, \' single quote.
immutability - Strings cannot be modified in place. Must create new string.
help(str) - Lists all available string methods in Python shell.

Topic: Dictionary

dict (overview) - Mutable key-value mapping collection. Keys must be unique and hashable.
dict[key] - Direct access to value by key (raises KeyError if missing).
get(key, default=None) - Safe access to value with optional default if key missing.
in - Checks if key exists in dictionary.
update(dict) - Adds or updates multiple key-value pairs in one operation.
setdefault(key, default) - Returns value if key exists, else adds key with default.
pop(key, default=None) - Removes key and returns its value (safe with default).
popitem() - Removes and returns the last inserted key-value pair.
del - Directly removes a key from the dictionary.
clear() - Removes all items from dictionary.
keys() - Returns an iterator over all dictionary keys.
values() - Returns an iterator over all dictionary values.
items() - Returns an iterator over all key-value pairs (most useful for looping).
copy() - Returns a shallow copy of the dictionary.
fromkeys(iterable, value) - Creates a new dict with keys from iterable, all values set to value.
len() - Returns the number of key-value pairs in dictionary.
merge (|) - Merges two dicts with union operator (Python 3.9+).
dict comprehension - Create dict from iterable using {k: v for k, v in iterable}.
nested dict - Dictionary containing other dictionaries as values.
use case - Ideal for API responses, configs, test metadata, key-value lookups.

Topic: Flow Control (if/elif/else)

if (overview) - Runs a block only when a condition is True.
if/else - Runs one block if True, another if False.
if/elif/else - Checks multiple conditions top to bottom; first True branch wins, rest skipped.
indentation - Python uses indentation (not braces) to define blocks; inconsistent indent = IndentationError.
comparison operators - ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= used to build conditions.
logical operators - and (both true), or (either true), not (flips value).
short circuit evaluation - In and/or chains, evaluation stops as soon as the result is known; remaining conditions don't run.
truthy/falsy - 0, "", [], {}, set(), None, False are falsy; everything else truthy. e.g. username = "" is falsy.
bool is int subclass - bool class is a subclass of int; True == 1 and False == 0.
nested if - if inside if; prefer combining with `and` to avoid deep nesting.
ternary expression - value = a if condition else b (compact one-line if/else).
pass - No-op placeholder used when a branch needs no code yet.
guard clause - Early `return` on invalid input to avoid deep nesting.
== vs is - == compares value equality; is compares identity (use for `is None`).

Topic: Loops (for, while)

for loop (overview) - Iterates over a known/finite sequence (list, string, range, dict).
range(start, stop, step) - Generates a sequence of numbers; stop is exclusive.
end= in print() - print(x, end=" ") changes line ending so loop output can print on one line.
enumerate() - Loops with both index and value together.
for over dict.items() - Loops through key-value pairs together.
while loop (overview) - Repeats while a condition stays True; used when iteration count is unknown.
while loop with else - else block runs only if the while condition ended naturally (no break hit).
while True (no condition) - Loops forever by design; must be controlled from inside via break/continue.
smart looping techniques - continue (skip iteration), pass (no-op placeholder), break (exit loop) - the 3 loop-control tools.
break - Exits the loop entirely.
continue - Skips only the current iteration, loop continues.
pass - No-op placeholder inside a loop body.
for/while...else - else block runs only if the loop completes without hitting break.
nested loops - Loop inside another loop, e.g. suites x tests combinations.
list comprehension - [x for x in items if condition] - compact filter/transform loop.
dict comprehension in loop context - {k: v for k, v in iterable} built from a loop-like expression.
common bug - Never mutate a list while iterating it directly; build a new list/comprehension instead.
